subject: DevOps
title: Multi-Cloud: Pros and Cons
description: A micro-lesson that makes Multi-Cloud: Pros and Cons usable.

hook.text: AWS us-east-1 went down. Single-cloud companies were stuck. Multi-cloud firms stayed online.

buildup.text: Multi-cloud uses two or more providers. It cuts vendor lock-in and survives provider outages.

discovery.text: The cost: complexity doubles. Each cloud has different APIs, networking, IAM, and pricing.

twist.text: True active-active multi-cloud is rare. Most firms pick best-of-breed per workload instead.

climax.text: Terraform and Kubernetes help abstract clouds. Terraform provisions anywhere. K8s runs anywhere.

punchline.text: Multi-cloud adds resilience but also complexity. Choose wisely.

quiz.question: What is the biggest challenge of a multi-cloud strategy?
options: Increased complexity from managing different APIs, networking, and tools per cloud
Cloud providers charge extra for multi-cloud setups
It's impossible to use Kubernetes across multiple clouds
